value_picks.name: Intuit Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $5.232B / market cap $70.51B = 7.42% FCF yield, clears the 7% pass bar.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 9.43 vs implied PE 16.84 (median target $460 / fwd EPS 27.32) = 44.0% discount, well above the 30% pass threshold.

drawdown.detail: Down 68.32% from 52w high of $813.70, far past the 35% pass bar — deepest drawdown in the set.

balance_sheet.detail: Roughly net-flat ($6.78B cash vs $6.90B debt, -$0.12B) but D/E 33.4 (<50) AND current ratio 1.45 (>1.2) clears the pass condition.

dividend.detail: Yield 1.86% is below the 2% pass cutoff; payout 28.3% is very safe, so qualifies as borderline (yield>0 AND payout<90%).

institutional.detail: Institutions hold 92.6% (>75%) and rec_mean 1.65 (<2.5); analyst dist 27 buy/strong-buy vs 6 hold, 1 sell.

ai_exposure.detail: Intuit is embedding GenAI assistants across QuickBooks, TurboTax and Credit Karma to automate bookkeeping, tax and advice — a margin/retention tailwind, though AI also risks commoditizing basic tax prep over time. Net positive given the proprietary financial data moat.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in the input. Short interest is modest at 4.36% (2.0 days to cover) and rose only 3.2% MoM — no notable positioning signal from the supplied fields.

catalysts_general: Trading at a 44% discount to the median analyst target of $460
Forward PE of 9.4 against 10.4% revenue growth and 10.7% earnings growth (PEG 0.67)
Price ($257.77) sitting just $5 above the 52-week low of $252.84, ~13% below the 20d SMA of $297.82
FCF yield of 7.4% with 47% operating margins and 22.5% ROE

downside_scenarios.scenario: Break below the 52w low toward the lowest analyst target ($275) overshooting — fwd PE 8.1
Multiple compresses to ~7x on a guide-down / macro de-rating of SMB software

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just below the 52-week low of $252.84 to confirm capitulation; at $248 the FCF yield rises to ~7.7% and the discount-to-target widens past 46%.

comparables.note: In-input Software-Application peer; INTU is cheaper on fwd PE despite higher institutional ownership
In-input peer growing faster but at 2x INTU's forward multiple
Not in input data — referenced as the classic high-margin app-software comp INTU trades against

value_picks.analyst_take: Intuit screens as the strongest name in the set at 5.5/6, combining a >7% FCF yield, a 44% discount to consensus target and a brutal 68% drawdown that has reset the multiple to 9.4x forward. The franchise (QuickBooks, TurboTax, Credit Karma) is high-margin and AI-advantaged; the only soft spot is a sub-2% dividend. BUY into the 52-week-low zone.

value_picks.analyst_take: Zoetis is a high-quality franchise (28% margins, 68% ROE, the only sustainable dividend in the set) trading at 10x forward and a 42% discount to target after a 53% drawdown. But D/E of 286 fails the balance-sheet test and revenue growth has stalled at 2.9%, so the 4.5/6 leans on cheapness and quality rather than momentum. WATCHLIST — attractive but not yet a table-pounding entry given the leverage and decelerating top line.

value_picks.analyst_take: Fox is statistically cheap — 8.7x forward, ~6.8% FCF yield, 30% discount to target — but the 4.5/6 masks deteriorating fundamentals: revenue is shrinking 8.6% and earnings fell 49%, and it carries the highest short interest in the set at 16%. The cheapness is a value trap risk without a growth catalyst, though live news/sports keep it AI-insulated. WATCHLIST — only compelling on a deeper pullback toward the low-$40s where the FCF yield more than compensates for the secular decline.
